case_title: CADLES OF GRASSY MEADOWS II, L.L.C. VS. PEGASOFT CORPORATION ET AL

DEFAULT JUDGMENT REJECTED, E-FILING REJECT, SUBMITTED BY PLAINTIFF CADLES OF GRASSY MEADOWS II, L.L.C. , A WEST VIRGINIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY AS TO DEFENDANT PEGASOFT CORPORATION , A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ARSHINKOFF, MARK T. , AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEFENDANT PEGASOFT CORPORATION'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND FOR LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED. THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST PEGASOFT ON OCTOBER 23, 2017 IS VACATED AND PEGASOFT MUST FILE ITS PROPOSED ANSWER NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 29, 2017. PEGASOFT HAS SHOWN THAT MISTAKE AND EXCUSABLE NEGLECT PER CCP 473(B) TO WARRANT SETTING ASIDE THE DEFAULT. GRANTING THE MOTION COMPORTS WITH THE STRONG POLICY OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS AND PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO IDENTIFY ANY LEGAL PREJUDICE IT WOULD SUFFER BY THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION.(AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT). COUNSEL FOR PEGASOFT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR# 7489, MGHENO@SFTC.ORG, REPORTED. (D302/HK)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Dec-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Assly Sayyar, Esq. 760-542-8717 for Plaintiff though Court Call.
No other appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PEGASOFT CORPORATION's MOTION TO SET ASIDE and/or Vacate DEFAULT and for leave to Defend Action.

Defendant PegaSoft Corporation's motion to set aside the default and for leave to amend is granted. The default entered against PegaSoft on October 23, 2017 is vacated and PegaSoft must file its proposed answer no later than December 29, 2017. PegaSoft has shown that mistake and excusable neglect per CCP 473(b) to warrant setting aside the default. Granting the motion comports with the strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and plaintiff has failed to identify any legal prejudice it would suffer by the granting of the motion.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for PegaSoft is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR# 7489, mgheno@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)
